item.title: Justin Bieber Brings Out Billie Eilish, SZA, & More At Coachella
item.dc:creator: Abby Jones
item.description:
Justin Bieber's Coachella Weekend 1 set has been spawning a lot of conversations online. Some people argue that him bringing out a laptop to sing karaoke over his old music videos was lazy; others argue that he's endured enough in his career and should be able to do whatever the hell he wants. Either way, he switched things up for his Weekend 2 headlining performance last night, bringing out a slew of special guests: Billie Eilish, SZA, Sexyy Red, Big Sean, and Dijon.

The post Justin Bieber Brings Out Billie Eilish, SZA, & More At Coachella appeared first on Stereogum.

item.title: Olivia Rodrigo Joins Addison Rae At Coachella For “drop dead” Live Debut & “Headphones On”
item.dc:creator: Abby Jones
item.description:
Yesterday was the first anniversary of Addison Rae's "Headphones On," one of last year's best songs. The day before yesterday, Olivia Rodrigo released "drop dead," the lead single to her upcoming third album you seem pretty sad for a girl so in love. This all coincided with Weekend 2 of Coachella, a festival that's become notorious for surprise guest appearances. Rae and Rodrigo seized the opportunity to link up during the former's Coachella set yesterday, performing both of those aforementioned songs together.

The post Olivia Rodrigo Joins Addison Rae At Coachella For “drop dead” Live Debut & “Headphones On” appeared first on Stereogum.

item.title: Tyler, The Creator Addresses Fans Leaking Security Camera Footage Of Him Again
item.dc:creator: Danielle Chelosky
item.description:
Surveillance culture: It sucks. Late last month, security camera footage of Tyler, The Creator at a bookstore in Mexico City was leaked online, and he addressed the situation today on his Instagram Story, lamenting, "i understand the excitment but the behavior is going to morph into everyone having footage or anything involving them uploaded for content."

The post Tyler, The Creator Addresses Fans Leaking Security Camera Footage Of Him Again appeared first on Stereogum.

item.title: Watch Ninajirachi Debut New Porter Robinson Remix With Underscores At Coachella
item.dc:creator: Danielle Chelosky
item.description:
Last weekend, Ninajirachi's effervescent Coachella set peaked when she brought out Porter Robinson to debut a new song seemingly called "WannaCry." Last night for round two, the rising Australian hyperpop darling was joined by her equally exciting peer Underscores to perform a remix of Robinson's 2024 tune "Perfect Pinterest Garden."

The post Watch Ninajirachi Debut New Porter Robinson Remix With Underscores At Coachella appeared first on Stereogum.

item.title: Pope – “John Thomas” & “Sick Minute” (Feat. Ratboys’ Julia Steiner)
item.dc:creator: Danielle Chelosky
item.description:
Big week for popes: LA indie rockers Pope are back with new singles from their upcoming record BFM. The band released “John Thomas” and “Sick Minute” yesterday, and the latter features Julia Steiner of Ratboys fame. “‘Sick Minute’ is about trying your hardest, just because it’s always good to give it your best shot,” bandleader Matt…

The post Pope – “John Thomas” & “Sick Minute” (Feat. Ratboys’ Julia Steiner) appeared first on Stereogum.

item.title: Sabrina Carpenter Brought Out Madonna At Coachella Weekend 2
item.dc:creator: Danielle Chelosky
item.description:
Last weekend, Sabrina Carpenter's huge Hollywood-themed performance was the centerpiece of Coachella. Last night, she made it even bigger by bringing out Madonna, who announced her new album Confessions II earlier this week.

The post Sabrina Carpenter Brought Out Madonna At Coachella Weekend 2 appeared first on Stereogum.

item.title: Magdalena Bay Announce Imaginal Disk Cinematic Companion Premiering At Tribeca
item.dc:creator: Danielle Chelosky
item.description:
In 2024, Magdalena Bay's sophomore album Imaginal Disk brought the avant-pop duo into stardom. Mica Tenenbaum and Matthew Lewin announced last year that they were turning it into a movie, calling it "our most ambitious undertaking thus far." Yesterday they officially announced the film, which will be premiering at Tribeca Film Festival in June.

The post Magdalena Bay Announce <em>Imaginal Disk</em> Cinematic Companion Premiering At Tribeca appeared first on Stereogum.

item.title: Here’s The Coachella 2026 Weekend 2 Livestream & Schedule
item.dc:creator: Chris DeVille
item.description:
Coachella 2026's first weekend brought us cameos from all kinds of celebs (and non-celebs), Geese's Justin Bieber cover, a Diplo-M.I.A. reunion, and more. The fest's second edition kicks off today, and it will once again be streaming all weekend long, this time with surprise addition Kacey Musgraves in the spot occupied by Jack White last week.

The post Here’s The Coachella 2026 Weekend 2 Livestream & Schedule appeared first on Stereogum.

item.title: Ice Spice Attacked At Hollywood McDonald’s
item.dc:creator: Chris DeVille
item.description:
Can't Ice Spice enjoy her McDonald's in peace? The rapper was attacked while dining at a Hollywood location of the fast food franchise early Wednesday morning, TMZ reports.

The post Ice Spice Attacked At Hollywood McDonald’s appeared first on Stereogum.

item.title: The 5 Best Songs Of The Week
item.dc:creator: Stereogum
item.category: The 5 Best Songs Of The Week
item.description:
Every week the Stereogum staff chooses the five best new songs of the week. The eligibility period begins and ends Thursdays right before midnight. You can hear this week’s picks below and on Stereogum’s Favorite New Music Spotify playlist, which is updated weekly. (An expanded playlist of our new music picks is available to members on Spotify and Apple Music, updated throughout the week.)

The post The 5 Best Songs Of The Week appeared first on Stereogum.

item.title: Glazyhaze – “Do You?”
item.dc:creator: Margaret Farrell
item.description:
The newest single from Italian shoegazers Glazyhaze is dizzying bliss. “Do You?” follows last November’s one-off song “Nirvana,” which followed their sophomore album SONIC from earlier in the year. “Do You?” has some cozy distortive fuzz that’s contrasted nicely with guitar melody and sores above it. Lyrically, there’s some gloomy happy-sad stuff going on: “I…

The post Glazyhaze – “Do You?” appeared first on Stereogum.

item.title: “drop dead”: Gorgeous
item.dc:creator: Chris DeVille
item.description:
It’s a hit. That’s my first takeaway from "drop dead." There’s a lot to say about the lead single from Olivia Rodrigo’s third LP, but first and foremost it does what a lead single is supposed to do, drilling itself into your brain immediately. After one listen this morning, the chorus was playing in my head on loop.

The post “drop dead”: Gorgeous appeared first on Stereogum.

item.title: Crash Of Rhinos – “Recurring”
item.dc:creator: Chris DeVille
item.description:
UK emo revival heroes Crash Of Rhinos are making their grand return next month with Logbook, their first album in 13 years. They told us all about it. Today, they’re following lead single “Figures Of Light” with an impassioned, anthemic new one called “Recurring.” If you like your emo fast and melodic, with a perfect…

The post Crash Of Rhinos – “Recurring” appeared first on Stereogum.

item.title: Madonna – “I Feel So Free”
item.dc:creator: Tom Breihan
item.description:
A couple of days ago, Madonna confirmed her long-teased plans to release a new album called Confessions II this summer. The new album is the sequel to her 2005 LP Confessions On A Dance Floor, the last truly beloved Madonna album. Once again, Madonna recorded it with dance producer Stuart Price. Today, she shared a…

The post Madonna – “I Feel So Free” appeared first on Stereogum.

item.title: Another European Kanye West Concert Canceled
item.dc:creator: Tom Breihan
item.description:
Even if you say you're very sorry for adapting Nazi imagery and making a song called "Heil Hitler," certain European countries might not be eager to invite you in. That's the lesson that Kanye West is learning right now. West has a European stadium tour coming up, and he was initially announced as the headliner for all three nights of London's Wireless Festival. But the UK government denied entry to West, and the festival was canceled. After French authorities voiced similar intentions, West announced that he would postpone a planned Marseilles show "until further notice." Today, a stadium in Poland scrapped plans for another Kanye West concert.

The post Another European Kanye West Concert Canceled appeared first on Stereogum.

item.title: WU LYF Rescheduling US Reunion Tour
item.dc:creator: Tom Breihan
item.description:
One week ago, the reunited Manchester indie rockers WU LYF released their long-awaited sophomore album A Wave That Will Never Break. It came out 15 years after their debut, and it's fucking awesome. One week from today, WU LYF were scheduled to kick off their first North American tour in more than a decade. People were psyched for it. I've never seen WU LYF live, but people have said rapturous things about their show, and the new LP is full of songs that seem guaranteed to go off in person. Unfortunately, visa issues have forced WU LYF to reschedule their touring plans.

The post WU LYF Rescheduling US Reunion Tour appeared first on Stereogum.

item.title: The Jungle Giants – “A Moment Like That”
item.dc:creator: Tom Breihan
item.description:
The singer looks like Walter White. That’s my big takeaway from the Joel Wilson-directed video for Australian band the Jungle Giants’ new single “A Moment Like That.” To be clear: Lead Jungle Giant Sam Hales doesn’t need to look like Bryan Cranston’s Breaking Bad antihero. He doesn’t have that craggy face or that cold stare.…

The post The Jungle Giants – “A Moment Like That” appeared first on Stereogum.

item.title: Ashley McBryde – “Lines In The Carpet”
item.dc:creator: Tom Breihan
item.description:
Indie rock bands aren’t the only acts who release a whole flood of advance singles before dropping their albums. Right now, Ashley McBryde, one of the greatest artists currently working in the Nashville country system, is using the same rollout strategy. McBryde has a new album called Wild coming out next month. I’ve heard it,…

The post Ashley McBryde – “Lines In The Carpet” appeared first on Stereogum.

item.title: S.G. Goodman Shares Studio Version Of Her Butthole Surfers Cover “Pepper”
item.dc:creator: Tom Breihan
item.description:
In 1996, the larger-than-life underground noise-rock goblins known as the Butthole Surfers randomly scored a freak alt-rock chart-topper with “Pepper,” a psychedelic half-rap experiment that sounded a lot like the music Beck was making around that time. The success of “Pepper” didn’t lead to any further success for the Buttholes, and the song plays a…

The post S.G. Goodman Shares Studio Version Of Her Butthole Surfers Cover “Pepper” appeared first on Stereogum.

item.title: Iron Maiden To Skip Rock And Roll Hall Of Fame Induction
item.dc:creator: Tom Breihan
item.category: Rock And Roll Hall Of Fame
item.description:
It's finally happening. After years of eligibility and two failed nominations, British heavy metal legends Iron Maiden are being inducted into the Rock And Roll Hall Of Fame. Metalheads worldwide have taken offense to the Hall's past Maiden snubs. In 2023, frontman Bruce Dickinson said that the Hall was "an utter and complete load of bollocks" and that he didn't want to be inducted. When the induction happens in November, Iron Maiden will not attend.

The post Iron Maiden To Skip Rock And Roll Hall Of Fame Induction appeared first on Stereogum.

item.title: Oxis – “Grey Mullet” & “Scup”
item.dc:creator: Margaret Farrell
item.description:
LA-based musician Oxis is a prolific powerhouse. The marine-themed electronica act, whose tracks are all named after sea creatures, released her eighth full-length album Oxis 8 this past January. She’s already back with two new singles “Grey Mullet” and “Scup.” “Scup” starts off driven by acoustic guitar, until Oxis adds shuffling rhythmic embellishments, braiding the…

The post Oxis – “Grey Mullet” & “Scup” appeared first on Stereogum.

item.title: Watch Interpol Debut Another New Song, “Wings On Fire,” In Albuquerque
item.dc:creator: Tom Breihan
item.description:
The perennially well-dressed New York rock veterans Interpol seem to have something new in the hopper. Last month, they played São Paolo and debuted an as-yet-unreleased new song called "See Out Loud." Right now, Interpol are between Coachella weekends, and they've used the past week to tour around the Southwest a little bit. On Thursday night, Interpol played Albuquerque's Revel Entertainment Center and debuted another new track, this one called "Wings On Fire."

The post Watch Interpol Debut Another New Song, “Wings On Fire,” In Albuquerque appeared first on Stereogum.

item.title: fakemink – “Night, Blooming Jasmine .”
item.dc:creator: Tom Breihan
item.description:
The strange and charismatic London rapper/producer fakemink had a huge breakout in 2025, and he was one of our favorite new artists of the year. This year, fakemink has been all over the place. His album Terrified is apparently on the way, and he released the absorbing EP The Boy who cried Terrified . earlier…

The post fakemink – “Night, Blooming Jasmine .” appeared first on Stereogum.

item.title: ADÉLA – “KGB”
item.dc:creator: Margaret Farrell
item.description:
I have never heard of ADÉLA, but her new single “KGB,” her first new music of 2026 and a glimpse of her forthcoming debut album, features some heavy hitter producer credits — 100 Gecs’ Dylan Brady, Blake Slatkin, the Dare. It’s provocative and catchy. The verses remind me of Bruno Mars’ “Uptown Funk” pre-chorus, and…

The post ADÉLA – “KGB” appeared first on Stereogum.

item.title: Groupie – “Swan Song”
item.dc:creator: Tom Breihan
item.description:
Frontwoman Ashley Kossakowski formed the New York-based band Groupie before she learned how to play her bass, and there’s a whole lot of steely-eyed determination in the band’s sound. Kossakowski and her band make big, bright, catchy alt-rock with a whole lot of hooks; I’d put their sound somewhere between Bully and the Beaches. They’ve…

The post Groupie – “Swan Song” appeared first on Stereogum.

item.title: Tyla – “She Did It Again” (Feat. Zara Larsson)
item.dc:creator: Margaret Farrell
item.description:
Tyla and Zara Larsson have teamed up for “She Did It Again,” the lead single from Tyla’s upcoming sophomore album A-Pop. It comes with a sensual, stylish music video that embraces the 2000s era of pop stars like Britney Spears, Shakira, and Beyoncé. The Epic Records labelmates rock beautifully draped fabrics amidst majestic winds and…

The post Tyla – “She Did It Again” (Feat. Zara Larsson) appeared first on Stereogum.

item.title: Former Yamantaka // Sonic Titan Guitarist Details Demoralizing Effort To Find An Audience
item.dc:creator: Danielle Chelosky
item.description:
The curtain between music fans and the music industry is falling. At SXSW last month, Billboard hosted a podcast Q&A with the founders Chaotic Good Projects, a marketing agency that utilizes digital astroturfing to help artists go viral. A few weeks later, singer-songwriter Eliza McLamb drew attention to the company in a Substack post. Wired followed…

The post Former Yamantaka // Sonic Titan Guitarist Details Demoralizing Effort To Find An Audience appeared first on Stereogum.
item.dc:creator: Scott Lapatine

item.title: Slayyyter – “Broke Bitch Free$tyle”
item.dc:creator: Tom Breihan
item.description:
Last month, the St. Louis artist Slayyter, who our own Chris DeVille once described as “normcore hyperpop,” released her album Wor$t Girl In America. I played it once or twice, liked it, and then found myself coming back to it again and again. It’s a frenzied, over-the-top take on mall-pop, like what might’ve happened if…

The post Slayyyter – “Broke Bitch Free$tyle” appeared first on Stereogum.

item.title: Roger Waters Calls David Draiman “Nazi Pig” In Open Letter To Billy Corgan
item.dc:creator: Margaret Farrell
item.description:
Disturbed frontman David Draiman is the latest guest on Billy Corgan's podcast The Magnificent Others, which recently hosted Courtney Love. For years, Draiman has been vocal about his positions in politics and music: He's an adamant supporter of Israel, going so far as to do a photo op signing IDK missiles.

The post Roger Waters Calls David Draiman “Nazi Pig” In Open Letter To Billy Corgan appeared first on Stereogum.

item.title: Nine Inch Noize Is Here, And It Rocks
item.dc:creator: Tom Breihan
item.description:
When Nine Inch Nails and Boys Noize announced their collaborative record Nine Inch Noize via Coachella billboard last week, we wondered what the end result might be: A live album? A remix collection? A whole new collaboration? Now, the album is out, and we’re still not quite sure what to call it. It’s a live…

The post <em>Nine Inch Noize</em> Is Here, And It Rocks appeared first on Stereogum.

item.title: Kacey Musgraves – “Middle Of Nowhere”
item.dc:creator: Margaret Farrell
item.description:
In a few weeks Kacey Musgraves will release her sixth studio album Middle Of Nowhere. We’ve already heard the lead single “Dry Spell,” which was one of our favorites from that week. Today, she’s shared the title track. “Middle Of Nowhere” starts in a soft stride. “Out there on the edge of the world, way…

The post Kacey Musgraves – “Middle Of Nowhere” appeared first on Stereogum.

item.title: Olivia Rodrigo – “Drop Dead”
item.dc:creator: Danielle Chelosky
item.description:
After lots of rumors and anticipation, Olivia Rodrigo announced her new album you seem pretty sad for a girl so in love a couple weeks ago. The Guts followup, produced by regular collaborator Daniel Nigro, arrives in June and its lead single “Drop Dead” is finally here. The pop wunderkind teased “Drop Dead” with murals…

The post Olivia Rodrigo – “Drop Dead” appeared first on Stereogum.

item.title: Watch Ethel Cain Cover Drive-By Truckers For Like A Version
item.dc:creator: Danielle Chelosky
item.description:
In 2021, Ethel Cain posted a demo called "Wrestling In Dirt Pits" to Tumblr, and it sampled Drive-By Truckers' "Angels And Fuselage" from their 2001 album Southern Rock Opera. Now the alt-pop singer covered the track for Triple J's Like A Version series.

The post Watch Ethel Cain Cover Drive-By Truckers For Like A Version appeared first on Stereogum.

item.title: D4vd Arrested In The Murder Of Teen Found Dead In His Tesla
item.dc:creator: Danielle Chelosky
item.description:
David Burke, the 21-year-old alt-pop singer who performs as D4vd, has been arrested for the murder of 14-year-old Celeste Rivas Hernandez, according to the Los Angeles Police Department.

The post D4vd Arrested In The Murder Of Teen Found Dead In His Tesla appeared first on Stereogum.
item.dc:creator: Scott Lapatine

item.title: Underscores Follows Set At Dry Cleaners With Guest-Filled U-Haul Pop-Up
item.dc:creator: Abby Jones
item.description:
Underscores’ great new album U arrived last month. As part of the album rollout festivities, the singer/songwriter/producer played a DJ set in a Los Angeles dry cleaners, mixing U highlights with seemingly everything from Rihanna to Janet Jackson to Boys Noize. It’s great! She followed that up by doing another pop-up DJ set in Los…

The post Underscores Follows Set At Dry Cleaners With Guest-Filled U-Haul Pop-Up appeared first on Stereogum.

item.title: Claire Rousay – “Hey Eleanor” (Casiotone For The Painfully Alone Cover)
item.dc:creator: Chris DeVille
item.description:
Way back in 2003, Owen Ashworth, who now records as Advance Base, released his album Twinkle Echo under his former moniker Casiotone For The Painfully Alone. That album contained a short, sweet track called “Hey Eleanor.” Wednesday, Claire Rousay released a cover of that song. According to her note on Bandcamp, she threw it together…

The post Claire Rousay – “Hey Eleanor” (Casiotone For The Painfully Alone Cover) appeared first on Stereogum.

item.title: Neko Case’s Thelma & Louise Musical Will Debut In London This Fall
item.dc:creator: Chris DeVille
item.description:
When we interviewed her last year ahead of new album Neon Grey Midnight Green, Neko Case said things were in motion with the Thelma & Louise musical she's been working on for a decade. Now it's finally going to debut.

The post Neko Case’s <em>Thelma & Louise</em> Musical Will Debut In London This Fall appeared first on Stereogum.

item.title: Lana Del Rey Shares Theme From New James Bond Game
item.dc:creator: Abby Jones
item.description:
No, we still don’t know when Stove is coming. Lana Del Rey’s long-delayed new album —initially announced as a country album called Lasso — remains an enigma. But it’s not that she refuses to put out new music: In February she dropped the single “White Feather Hawk Tail Deer Hunter.” Today she’s back with “First…

The post Lana Del Rey Shares Theme From New James Bond Game appeared first on Stereogum.

item.title: Boards Of Canada Share First New Music In 13 Years
item.dc:creator: Abby Jones
item.description:
Boards Of Canada fans were in a tizzy last week after some people received mysterious VHS tapes in the mail from Warp, their longtime label. Those tapes featured some haunting, static-y audio, not necessarily a song, but it felt like pretty solid evidence that the IDM icons might be gearing up for a big return.…

The post Boards Of Canada Share First New Music In 13 Years appeared first on Stereogum.

item.title: Charli XCX Says She’s Making A Rock Album
item.dc:creator: Tom Breihan
item.description:
"I think the dancefloor is dead, so now we’re making rock music." That's the money quote, and apparently it's a lyric. Charli XCX is the subject of a new British Vogue cover story, mostly about her work on the proper follow-up to her career-exploding 2024 landmark Brat. Charli has been extremely busy since Brat. She just released her Wuthering Heights soundtrack album in February, and she's acting in approximately one million movies this year, but apparently she's had time to work on another record amid all of that.

The post Charli XCX Says She’s Making A Rock Album appeared first on Stereogum.
